# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Release Workflow
on:
  push:
    tags:
      - '*'
jobs:
  build_and_release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2
      
      # Build project for different languages (Node.js, Python)
      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v1
        with:
          node-version: 14
          
      - name: Install and build
        run: |
          npm ci
          npm run build
      
      # Publish to NPM
      - name: Login to NPM
        run: npm login
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
          
      - name: Publish package to NPM
        run: npm publish
      
      # Create GitHub release for tag pushes
      - name: Create Release
        id: create_release
        uses: actions/create-release@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          
       # Publish packages to PyPI for Python projects
      - name: Publish Package to PyPI
        uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1
        with:
          user: __token__
          password: ${{ secrets.PYPI_API_TOKEN }}
